title: Future Value Calculator - Compound Interest FV
shortDescription: Calculate the future value of a lump sum with compound interest.
description: Find out how much a one-time investment will grow over time. This future value calculator applies compound interest to a present value using the formula FV = present value × (1 + rate/100)^years.
metaDescription: Free future value calculator. Enter a present value, annual rate and number of years to see how a lump sum grows with compound interest.

result.label: Future value
result.empty: Enter a present value, an annual rate and a number of years to see the future value.

sections.heading: What is future value?
paragraphs: Future value (FV) is what a sum of money invested today will be worth at a future date, assuming it grows at a fixed compound rate.
Because interest is earned on both the original amount and on previously earned interest, the balance grows faster over longer periods.

sections.heading: The formula
paragraphs: This calculator uses FV = present value × (1 + rate/100)^years, where the rate is the annual interest rate as a percentage and years is the number of years invested.

howTo.name: Enter the present value
howTo.text: Type the amount you are investing or saving today.

howTo.name: Set the annual rate
howTo.text: Enter the expected annual interest or growth rate as a percentage.

howTo.name: Enter the years
howTo.text: Type how many years the money will stay invested to see the future value.

faq.a: It computes the future value of a single lump sum that grows with annual compound interest.
Interest is compounded once per year using FV = present value × (1 + rate/100)^years.

faq.q: Can I use a decimal rate?
faq.a: Yes. You can enter rates like 4.5 or 7.25 for more precise results.
No. The result is a nominal amount and does not adjust for inflation or taxes.
With a 0% rate the future value equals the present value, because no growth is applied.
